decisions.title: Local Snapshot Authority
decisions.statement: spawn is the authoritative local snapshot builder for state_space.
rationale: Only local runtime can observe host-specific user/system state faithfully.
Gate decisions require deterministic host-local evidence.
implications: Snapshot outputs are immutable artifacts keyed by snapshot_id.
Primary state-space generation does not move to GitHub-hosted runners.

decisions.title: Runtime Surface as Declared Ownership Primitive
decisions.statement: runtime_surface is the authoritative declared contract for one managed app, service, binary, wrapper, or launcher.
rationale: Runtime ownership must remain explicit even when no file-declared settings exist.
Config files, wrappers, units, and launchers are rendered outputs, not authority.
implications: Every managed runtime surface is schema-bound.
userland_spec is an aggregate over runtime_surface[] rather than the base declared unit.

decisions.title: GitHub as Secondary Enforcement Plane
decisions.statement: GitHub workflows re-validate and enforce published artifacts, but do not become the primary state-space builder.
rationale: Remote runners cannot observe local runtime truth with sufficient fidelity.
Artifact-based verification is useful downstream of local state generation.
implications: CI blocks merges on INVALID or stale UNKNOWN outcomes.
CI consumes already-published artifacts rather than probing host state directly.

decisions.title: Codex Excluded from Authoritative Build Path
decisions.statement: codex-cli is not placed in the authoritative snapshot build path.
rationale: It adds nondeterminism, auth/secrets burden, and external runtime failure modes to the gate path.
implications: Codex may assist with advisory work, but not authoritative state assembly.

decisions.title: Query Then Policy
decisions.statement: Impact/query computation derives deterministic facts first; OPA/Rego evaluates policy over those facts second.
rationale: Separates fact derivation from policy decisions.
Keeps policy canonical in git and outside query text.
implications: Gate output includes decision, reasons, snapshot_id, and policy_bundle_sha.
Policy input remains compact and structured.

decisions.title: DuckDB First, Graph DB by Evidence
decisions.statement: The initial graph/query stack is spawn + DuckDB + OPA sidecar; a graph database is adopted only with demonstrated need.
rationale: Keeps the first implementation simpler and easier to audit.
Graph-specific adoption should be justified by traversal complexity or performance evidence.
implications: SQL/CTE derivation is the baseline path.

decisions.title: Hash-Chained Snapshot Evidence
decisions.statement: Snapshot artifacts use hash-chaining and optional signatures for tamper evidence.
rationale: Provides auditability with lower operational complexity than a ledger/blockchain design.
implications: Published snapshots carry prev_hash and integrity evidence.

decisions.title: Direct/Enrich Canonical Ingress
decisions.statement: Userland live-state ingress uses a single canonical event contract with direct and enrich modes before reduction.
rationale: Different triggers may or may not carry enough information to mutate state directly.
A single canonical event shape prevents pipeline divergence.
implications: Both modes must emit the same live_state_event contract.
Schema validation occurs at the write boundary.
Events carry origin, evidence_ref, and idempotency metadata.

decisions.title: Dumb LOOP, Spawn-Owned Transition Actuation
decisions.statement: LOOP remains a dumb packet lifecycle shell; spawn owns signal normalization, transition validation, reconciliation, and actuation between local FSM state and GitHub projection.
rationale: Skill-spec compliance is preserved when phase skills and LOOP emit/consume artifacts instead of embedding orchestration logic.
Runtime eventing and actuation belong in the daemon, not in the skill layer.
implications: LOOP owns $wt and packet lifecycle artifacts but not transition semantics.
spawn becomes the authoritative local transition engine.

decisions.title: systemd.path as Local Raw Signal Substrate
decisions.statement: Local artifact raw signals are detected via systemd .path units scoped to $wt and bridged to spawn through oneshot services.
rationale: systemd.path provides event-driven local artifact detection without a resident polling loop.
Separating raw detection from semantic interpretation keeps path units simple and spawn authoritative.
implications: Artifact writes under $wt become the local event ingress plane.
Oneshot bridge units normalize observations and hand them to spawn.
